title: Mexican Cream Chicken Stew
description: A rich and comforting stew featuring chicken in a creamy, miso-based sauce. Serve with warm tortillas or over rice.
instructions: Season the chicken with salt, pepper, and cumin. Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large cazuela over medium-high heat.
Brown the chicken on all sides, about 5-7 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.
In the same cazuela, add 1 more tablespoon of oil and cook the onion and garlic until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes, chicken broth, and miso paste to the cazuela, stirring to combine.
Return the chicken to the cazuela and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce heat to medium-low and let cook, covered, for 20-25 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
Stir in the heavy cream and let cook for an additional 2-3 minutes or until heated through.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro and warm tortillas on the side.
Refrigerate or freeze leftovers for later use.
